message:
feat(autograd): persistent gradient accumulation across backward passes

Implement always-on gradient accumulation in TensorTape that survives
tape consumption, enabling microbatch training without external bookkeeping.

crates/ft-autograd/src/lib.rs:
- New persistent_grads: BTreeMap<usize, Vec<f64>> field on TensorTape
- tensor_accumulated_gradient: read persistent gradient for a node
- tensor_accumulated_gradient_values: clone to owned Vec
- zero_tensor_accumulated_gradient: zero entries in-place (idempotent)
- accumulate_persistent_gradients: called at end of every backward pass,
  element-wise adds into existing entries or inserts new ones
- Hook into tensor_backward immediately after gradient computation

crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs:
- tensor_accumulated_gradient: forwarding wrapper on FrankenTorchSession
- tensor_zero_grad: zero one node's persistent gradient
- tensor_zero_grads: zero a slice of nodes

crates/ft-optim/src/lib.rs:
- All 6 optimizers (SGD, Adam, AdamW, RMSprop, Adagrad, RAdam) now read
  from persistent store via load_param_gradient instead of backward report
- zero_grad now calls zero_param_gradients (was no-op), matching standard
  PyTorch training loop pattern
- Tests updated: zero_grad_is_noop -> sgd_zero_grad_clears_persistent_gradients
- New tests: sgd_accumulates_gradients_across_backwards (3x backward on
  x^2, expects 3*2*4=24.0), sgd_step_reads_persistent_gradients_not_report_payload,
  sgd_microbatch_accumulation_matches_single_batch_step (4 microbatches)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
